review_text:
Will opt to sleep in my car over hiring a cabin here ever again.
I have read reviews stating that it's been maintained and upgraded - not sure what cabins they're speaking of but no.

I tried speaking with the man at reception to make friendly conversation and was met with cold hostility. Truly regret my accomodation choice but unfortunately, the situation was dire and if you need 4 walls and a roof, that's all you will get for an overpriced amount.

The cabin I stayed in was awfully horrendous. The first thing that hits you is the odour - dank, musty and hard to breathe.
Walking just 3 steps from the door to the airconditoner, there were things stuck on my feet from the unclean flooring.
The a/c was filthy and the flow out of it was only *just* bearable in comparison to the actual smell of the cabin.

Bathroom was abhorrent standards. Don't even get me started on that debacle. There were thongs on the floor outside the bathroom, presumably from a previous visitor who fled and forgot their shoes, or to kindly prevent potential tinea 😷

The Tv had 0 value and was entirely for decorative purpose so it was considered "basic standards" - no antenna so no channels available.

The beds? - literally had a sheet on them. No blankets.
No pillows.
Nothing.

The lights worked, so there's that.

1 night cost $130 on top of my Sanity.

If I could give it 0 stars - I would.

Disgraceful and appalling.
In desperate need of a good clean and tidy up.  The park does not reflect or match the internet photos. The only good thing about it was location.  Just off the highway.

I wouldn't recommend this caravan park to anyone. It is a small dingy park that isn't worth the $50 fee for a powered site. There was a smell of sewage coming out of the ground next to our site that we couldn't escape. We have two small dogs that we had to keep locked in the van because of the number of cats running loose in the park. Avoid at all cost. Cheap fuel at service station next to the park was the only positive.

Worst caravan park in NSW and I have stayed at many, old photos on website not at all like the park is now, very embarrassing to have international travels in campervans arriving at this park, not at all up to standard for a caravan park,I had a very disappointing stay and also paid far to much for what was on offer.
Place is lo g i n the tooth

When you can hear the smoke alarm flat battery beep when you book over the phine, its an accurate indicator of the poor qaulity of the facility. I wouldnt do a negative review if the price was cheaper, though for what i paid $160 for, it warrents my feedback. I wouldve slept in my car if i knew how run down the cabin was. You can see the fake reviews here with basic pattern recognition. How health and safety allow these places to trade is odd.
If you need somewhere to put your head down for the night, it's fine, but if you are looking for anything extra, drive on.

Our first experience in the cabin was very good.
Despite the cabin’s own noises, nothing that bothered or disturbed anyone’s peace.

We returned this past weekend and had a rather disturbing experience. There was loud music and shouting in the nearby cabins and tents until almost 2 a.m., with no supervision at all. The place was dirty and missing basic kitchen utensils.

To make things worse, we had personal belongings stolen — a loss of over 2,000 AUD. There were no security cameras, and we have no idea who might have entered the cabin during the short time we were away.

I appreciate the effort from the manager and the cleaning lady who tried to locate the stolen items. However, I believe the very least would be to have proper security within a closed facility.

Be careful if you plan to visit this place — unfortunately, we couldn’t count on the management for support.

-In response to the management’s comment:

This is not a case of misplaced personal belongings. High-value items were stolen from inside the cabin, which was locked inside the park. There were no double deadbolt locks on the door, contrary to what was stated in response to my feedback.

Interestingly, after I submitted my feedback, I received a phone call informing me that my documents — which were inside the stolen bag — were found in Port Macquarie. We did not travel to that area, which indicates that the person who took the items was staying at the Caravan Park and, upon returning to that region, discarded the documents there.

Security within the park is the responsibility of management. The police have already been notified, and you will be held accountable for this incident.

We recently stopped in for a night at the bell haven on our drive to qld from Vic, the staff were so accommodating from the moment we pulled nn without a reservation,  we were shown the rooms available before deciding to take the newer of two cabins. The cabins were clean comfortable and perfect for what we needed. The secure front electric gates was perfect for our needs as we had a load of personal belongings on the ute. Was a seamless check in and check out. Very highly recommended this caravan park. Thanks you for the good service , we will stay here again

I would not let my animals stay at this park. Red flag at office which smelt so bad I almost vomited, it is disgusting and I’m not sure how health inspectors have not closed it down. When booking they warn you there is no refund and when you get to park you will understand why as when you see the filth and state of park you would ask for refund, and charge $45 for powered site. AVOID

Stay far FAR away as possable from this establishment.
When you come to check-in you either use an intercom to communicate with the staff inside or if they invite you in it stinks like smelly feet and has stuff everywhere.
The room is stayed in had see though curtains and was concerned someone would peep inside.
The TV was placed is such a weird spot.
The shower head was half coming off and I HAD to fix it if I wanted a shower, bring your own amenities as i got body was only and when asking for more they said I had to pay extra after asking what they said like 7 times.
Not recommended!

Dirty and lots of rubbish, no trees no plants no garden no pool! Everything old and ugly, but you still can pay $45-$60 per night! Do yourself a favour and go somewhere else. This place should be shut down.
We have stayed here before customer service very good and the rooms are newly serviced and good budget.  Will be back again.

Cabin roomy, warm. Reasonable layout. Pretty good location, very little highway noise. Shower water pressure pretty good, water hot. Bed reasonably comfy. However..... no dishcloth,  no washing up detergent, no plug for the kitchen sink. There is an expectation that anything you use is to be washed, dried and put away etc to avoid a cleaning charge, however you are not supplied the tools. No washer in the bathroom, taps on the basin are back to front. Overall, reasonable value, but these little thing could improve the value immensely.

This place is crap keep driving find somewhere else $45 and night for this dump wow
Its sad to say but this is a 3rd world caravan park
I even put a complaint in with the local council
Notting a bulldozer could not fix
Try the local show grounds would be much cleaner and less money
WHAT A DUMP

Excellent customer service, especially from Ahmad Ramy. The rooms were clean, the location was convenient, and the price was very reasonable.

10/10 – probably one of the best caravan parks in the area.
Highly recommended!

OMG!! Disgusting doesn't describe the place. More like a trailer park. I feel really sorry for the residents. They must have nowhere else to go. We paid $180 for a cockroach infested 'cabin'. The fridge stank, the decor was awful. The linen did appear to be clean. No security door. I heard someone at the front door. Got our money back though, so not a complete disaster. I will be speaking with the local council tomorrow.

The reception being tucked behind a petrol station probably should’ve been the first red flag. We booked a $125 non-refundable cabin, only to find ourselves in what looked and felt like the park from 8 Mile.

The woman at the front desk was friendly, which was one of the few positives, but the park itself was completely run down. Our cabin had a mouldy shower curtain, a bathroom drain full of hair, and an overall feeling of neglect.

If the price matched the quality, it would be easier to accept, but at $125, it felt like a bit of a scam. We ended up leaving at 4 a.m. after being repeatedly woken by people loitering near the front of our cabin.

Definitely wouldn’t return or recommend.

Only stay here if you can't find anything else
Really need to clean up caravan park and office not a welcoming area 😒 cabin was really surprising but don't look at the finish product because it would be unsafe
Won't be staying here again
If you need a big fridge and decent TV for your overnight stay  this is the place.

Filthy toilets not cleaned along with anything else during my 5 day stay. $45 a night for a 17ft van site with 1 prrson and the place is putrid. No cleaning at all done for 5 days. The manager wasn't even offering a receipt until it was insisted upon||Keep driving folks .
Clean room. Great bed. Great shower. Noisy and smelly location behind a service station.

This is one of the best caravan parks in the region. You can have powered sites, short term stays as well as long term stays. The environment is great. You can see lots of birds flying.

There is a bus stop just in front of the Bellhaven Caravan Park. And there is a gas station too. If you want groceries, it is just 2km travel to Raymond Terrace. So everything is convenient here.

Terrible conditions, nothing like the photos very run down and unclean. Refused to stay there and returned the keys however the have accused me of keeping the keys even though we didn't even stay there!
$45 for a non-powered or a powered site!? Friendly staff but site was more like a construction site, or it's a work in progress possibly.

So far every room iv been in is super dirty and bed bugs everywhere DO NOT STAY HERE ALL YOUR PROPERTY WILL BE INFESTED WITH BUGS WHICH ARE IMUNE TO MOST POISIONS 100% UNSAFE AND ITS NOT REAL HEALTHY KEEP KIDS AWAY FROM THERE IT WILL END IN HOSPITAL TRIP.
This would have to the most disgusting place I have ever stayed, if I wasn’t so tired I would of left just gross

Stayed here for a few days, on sunday lunchtime one of the locals  who live in there "older guy with beard" caught him busting into my cabins window while i was in there he noticed me and took off! If i wasnt there i guess all my stuff would of been stolen!

Shouldn't be open to the public. Charges top dollar when shouldn't even pay half.
1) Hot water not work so instead of fixing runs hose to another cabin
2) cockroaches through cabin.
3) walls and all need good cleaning, top of fridge even covered in grease and dust settle.
4) unable to close bedroom doors without a straight(hits bed or drags on floor)
5)Feral cats through park meowing all hrs of night.
6)mould throughout communal showers/toilets.
7) smell of sewer round centre of park near dump point etc.
8)old food wrappers etc still in cupboards.
9) Office more resemble a workshop/maintenance shed.
10) flooring has lips etc and cause trip hazards and feel as if going to come up/fall.

Hellhaven dirty scruffy like a war zone
Big mistake huge staying here

We booked a drive through site for one night. We drove to our site and immediately began looking for somewhere else to stay. The place is so rundown, and doesn't appear to have money spent on upgrades for the past twenty plus years. And that's being generous. No pool. Looks nothing like the pictures. We were extremely disappointed. We had no choice but to pay for the unused night but there was no way we were staying. The one star is for location and the politeness of the lady at the desk. Never going back.

Omg , we definitely didn't do our  research with this stay . What a tip , as you drive in to check in , your met with the most disappointing feeling ever . |We had paid already online with no refunds,  other wise a very fast u turn and would be better off just sleeping in the car . Stayed in one of the cabins,  and wasn't sure if our car would still be there in the morning.  Not a pleasant stay at all , no sure how this place is still open. |Please don't waste your money,  keep driving to any where else.
還行
Not good. Sorry but there wasn’t a swimming pool as advertised  and there was rubbish and piles of dirt everywhere. Maybe it was under renovation but it was not worth the money at that time.

This place is the worst park we have ever stayed in, or anyone has ever stayed in.||We were there in our Caravan for 1 night and as we drove in my stomach sank and I said to my wife ‘Now you know why I put that lock on the inside of our caravan door’||This place should be reported to the Building Authorities and the Health Department as everything there, especially the on-site living cabins, are an absolute disgrace and beyond repair.||I would report them but I would hate to see the permanent residents left with no place to go. ||In my 7 year old daughter words ‘This place smells like Dookie’

As previous visitors have commented, this place is a disgusting mess.  As soon as I drove in, after I paid $50 for one night, absolute ripoff, I was confronted with a pile of rubbish outside a caravan that may or may not be lived in.   And it gets worse.  The amenities are dilapidated and dirty.  Dirty floor, handbasins, missing tiles, no soap, dust so it's obviously not cleaned every day.  Half of the park resembles a dump, dirty vans and rubbish everywhere, and the dump point?  A wheelie bin stuck in the ground with a hose next to it.  I shudder to think what you'd see if you opened the lid.  |How are they allowed to operate like this?  I went from paying $50 to stay in a dump to staying in a very clean showground for $25 then a lovely caravan park on the river costing $24.  I think their trick was when I tried to book, they told me to call back later when I'm arriving.  That way they knew I needed somewhere to stay so they charged $50.
No Complaints this way ..

Just spent three nights in our van in this park. It is exceptionally run-down and no money has been spent on anything other than mower fuel, toilet paper and soap for many, many years. But in saying that, our site was huge, level and mowed. As mentioned there was toilet paper and soap in the old, but clean, amenities block. The park is very quiet and suited us for our two days exploring up and down the coast. All onsite structures are run down, the pool is empty and the enclosure a mess and the clotheslines completely rusted and not fit for purpose. (Ruined my sheet!!!) In your own accommodation the park is fine, we had 3 awesome nights sleep, but anything onsite needs to be re-thought. A shame really. A few dollars, some love, a bulldozer and a few dozen skips and the place would do well. (Please just start with some clothesline wire from Bunnings!)

DO NOT I repeat DO NOT go there
We pulled in for one night with our caravan on a powered site We’d booked and paid online (Should have read the reviews) The caravan park is run down and looks like a tip with hard rubbish laying everywhere The smell was horrendous The showers were missing tiles on floors and walls ,broken shelving , and graffiti
I gave them one star because the shower I used had good pressure and the water was hot

This is the worst caravan park I have ever visited. |There were large piles of hard rubbish strewn all over the park. |The amenities had tiles missing in the shower cubicles - in all of them - and missing soap holders and broken shelves to place toiletries on. |No toilet paper in three toilet cubicles. |Rubbish overflowing from bins and more rubbish left alongside the bins. |The whole park was horrible. |A lot of seemingly permanent patrons were living in portables and a large number of what looked like unroadworthy motor vehicles were scattered around the park. |And yet, you still got charged $47:00 a night for a powered site - the same price as very clean respectable caravan parks. ||If you want cleanliness, tidy amenities and tidy rubbish bins which are emptied regularly, then do not come to this park. ||My score of ‘terrible’ is too high for this park. ||The owners should be ashamed of themselves. ||Honestly it is a disgrace.

Worst caravan park I’ve stayed at for an on-site van. Amenities were very old and in need of repairs. Whole place was run down and the large septic tank had awful odour. Can’t believe how it can stay operating in such a run down condition.

A run down dump. Once you got to speak to someone they where polite but had to wait ages to check in. The office stank of curry or something and was a filthy mess. Nice big cabin but dead cockroaches in cupboards.  Looked nicely layed out with a packet of biscuits and toiletries.  But couldn't wait to leave. Alot of people living there as well. Looks like family. Shame could be ok if cleaned up. Stayed 24th with small dog.

DO NOT STAY HERE - photos are very, very old and the park/including the office area are extremely run down and unclean.  Dump point was a council wheelie bin stuck in the ground next to the septic tanks in the middle of the park.  Felt unsafe staying here.|Rated 1 as the only good point - it had drive through sites.

The manager was very accommodating
Our car had broken down and we needed a room For the night
We got to our cabin and the bathroom had not been cleaned
Urine on  the toilet
Hair on the floor
2 rings left in the shower from previous guests
So we went and told him and he gave us another Cabin which was bigger and a little cleaner than the other one but definitely not worth the $160 for the night
Nothing was hygienically clean
Hair in shower
Stove top with fat splatters all over it
Glasses and mugs not cleaned properly
DISGUSTING. $120 for the most revolting van. It stunk, no hot water in bathroom sink. Shower filthy, the whole place was filthy. Cats and rubbish everywhere.

Terrible place , definitely would not recommend at all to stay here . Very ignorant receptionist when checking in. Cabin filthy inside nothing like the pictures online. Dirty bed sheets , dirty towels , dirty toilet , dead bugs everywhere on the floor. 30mm gap in front door which let cold air in all night. Do not stay here , keep driving.
A basic caravan park, not many sites but are all drive thru. Staff were very accommodating on arrival.

we drove in to our site went to amenities then drove out. we paid before hand but didn’t care we lost that money. what an absolute terrible park would never recommend to anyone. Drove 20 minutes up road and stayed elsewhere. DIDNT feel safe at that park.

Because it was run by folk who could speak VERY limited English, it was difficult to communicate.  I left behind my pyjamas and have not been able to get through to them.  After promising to get someone to contact me, I have heard nothing.
Rooms were good!
Fuel prices are always better than anywhere else nearby
Very few caravan sites, but dog friendly, we just stop for overnight and this was ok.

Very run down, lots of rubbish lying around. Mostly permanent residents. Amenities old & dated but clean. No soap dispenser in the ladies. Dump point was just a wheelie bin sunk in the ground, no hose just a tap next to it. Looked all around site but couldn’t find the pool. The few sites they had were drive through which was handy so ok just for an overnight stop but pricey at $45. Lady in the office was very nice

We found the staff helpful when we arrived during heavy rain. We requested accommodation for two and a dog. While the cabin was old it was okay. It was a handy stopover close to the interchange and no heavy traffic noise.

Unfortunately we didn't check the reviews before booking. We assumed the price reflected other choices of motels etc in the area and we needed a stop over.
The office was empty when arriving but the staff arrived quickly and were very nice and helpful but unfortunately.....
The website described a lovely Villa with pool and BBQ.
We paid online and were horrified when we got there. The place is a dump.
Permanent residents have squalor and the "Villa" we booked was a very basic trailer.
It's a simple room, bugs included, the pool was derelict and pad locked, the BBQ unusable.
The toilet was broken as the lid wouldn't stay up, blinds mouldy but at least a decent TV, microwave and tea/coffee supplies.
Was definitely not worth $135 a night.
Just one piece of advice, pull down your pants before lifting the toilet seat and sit quickly!

Bed bugs and a room that smells of cigarettes!

Lady at the front counter didn’t seem fussed when we came back up to make a complaint about the state of the room.

We asked for a refund for our room but again the lady at the counter did not want to cooperate with us. She said “we booked the room today, no refund”.

Booked last minute, thought great somewhere to stay, as our car had broken down 5 mins down the road. Get there and I walk into a cobweb when entering the room. There is little tiny bugs on the bed, and the whole room stank like smoke. Worst place, do not stay here. Wouldn’t even give me a refund because I booked it the same day as arriving.
Needed petrol. Good price. Can't say much else.

Did not go to caravan park went to servo for fuel.
Fuel was cheap  $1.96 for desiel
Map says name to go left or right to - NO SUCH NAME ON SIGNAGE!!! TOO OFTEN this IS THE CASE!
Would not recommend , we only had a very expensive powered site. Looking at the outside of rooms you wouldn’t go in. The whole place was a pig sty
Nice and clean room 👌

Excellent service a modern large clean cabin.
Staff were excellent, will stay again

It’s tired and very messy but the service was excellent helping us book. The powered sites are large and allow for parking over night while hooked up.  The park looks to be under going renovation or a clean as there was stuff everywhere. The shower and toilets need a serious clean up and refresh. It’s a good stop point at a good price point and provides a safe place to park and sleep overnight. Looking forward to returning again to check out what’s been done.
No choice. It was late. Had to stay here............no thank you
Needs hot food

I gave them 4 star as we booked last min. And they were very helpful and our cabin was really good. And pet friendly  too. Clean, modern amenities in the cabin and comfortable bed.  Cabin was in a quiet spot too.  Needs few little things in the cabin but for overnite stay it was great!
Clean and Peaceful. just wow !

Don't stay here. $50 per night and the place is a real dive. A rip off. Mostly long term worker accommodation by the looks of it. Mozzie infested. Very small with lots of cars. Noisy as right on highway. Toilets and showers were the highlight but even then pretty basic.
Price was reasonable, cabin was clean only down side was the drunks that came back late making a bit of a racket, otherwise all good

Driving into the park you feel like you've made a mistake.It looks more like a builders yard.Without doubt it is the worst park we have been in.We both decided not to shower as a lot of the tiles were missing.Sorry but I could not say a good word about this park.
When in cattle county you got to have the 🥩🥩. A lovely tasty evening 😚
4wd friendly hiflow diesel bowser pump always competitive
Not impressed with the Smell of sewage smell around the camp
Used the cheap petrol station, just off the highway, worth checking their prices

Very basic,sites are powered and have water but thats it,needs a all round clean up, showers had good pressure which is a bonus but in need of a renovation ,only there for a night wouldn't recommend it

We always choose to stay at Bellhaven. It is the perfect halfway for our many trips Brisbane to Canberra. Amenities are old, but always clean. Owners are lovely and booking is easy. Cheap overnight prices and very cheap petrol. We have a caravan and book a powered site. Easy drive through access. Plenty of eateries in walking distance so you don't have to unhook, just unplug and drive off in the morning. So convenient. All their cabins look brand new but haven't ever booked them. Infrastructure is old and is gradually being upgraded, but if you want modern and fancy, don't stay here. Ignore rude reviews if they had to wait for the owner to appear, you bet they arrived late while the family were having dinner or cleaning the amenities. Your kids will love chasing the bunnies in the morning!

This is a great park to stay if you have a caravan and want to just leave the van hooked up. Close to the highway and within walking distance to eating places. Very quiet and allows dogs. The park is very basic but great for a stop on the way to QLD. Staff very friendly.

Very disappointing stopped in over night after long drive. Checked in after 7 minutes waiting at the door. After big drive looking forward to a shower and wow 5 showers and only one working and amenities disgusting 🤢. Got over that and off to bed for the night and that wasn't good the septic smell was bad cost 45 that's fine put not for something in this state needs to be shut down.

DON"T just a run down junk yard (the old style demolition yard) 20 yr old Donna's, broken bed , nice enough people just have no idea how to maintain/run a caravan yard , sorry guys, you just need to spend some money and update the accommodation and yard
